description_long: This is a large, cylindrical steel barrel with a weathered blue paint finish, showing significant rust and grime, especially around the top and seams. The barrel features two red screw caps on the top surface, one larger and centrally located, the other smaller and offset. A prominent red and white label, partially visible, is affixed to the side, suggesting branding or hazardous material warning. The surface is dented and scratched, indicating heavy use and exposure to the elements, typical of barrels found in industrial or urban outdoor settings. Reinforcing ribs encircle the body, providing structural strength. The overall appearance suggests it is used for storing or transporting liquids such as oil, chemicals, or waste.
description: A weathered blue steel barrel with rust, two red screw caps on top, and a red and white label on the side. The surface is dented and reinforced with horizontal ribs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main red and white label, rust streaks, and both red screw caps on the top.
description_view_1: Side view with the larger red cap more visible, label partially out of sight, and heavy rust around the rim.
description_view_2: Rear view with both caps visible, label not visible, and pronounced rust and grime on the top edge.
description_view_3: Angled view highlighting the label, both caps, and extensive surface wear and rust.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: red and white label with partial text
